etymology.description: 粽(粽) (zòng, "sticky rice wrapped in bamboo leaves"), 子(子) (zi, "child; small thing")
etymology.interpretation: 粽(粽) (zòng, "sticky rice wrapped in bamboo leaves") refers directly to the traditional food item of sticky rice cooked in leaves. 子(子) (zi, "child; small thing") is often used in Chinese to denote a small or compact version of an object, essentially referring to a 'dumpling' or small parcel of food. Putting these together, 粽子 refers to the dish known as a rice dumpling, small packages of sticky rice encased in leaves, especially popular during the Dragon Boat Festival.

parts.english: sticky rice wrapped in bamboo leaves

examples.chinese: 端午节的时候(端午節的時候)，我们吃粽子(我們吃粽子)。
examples.pinyin: Duānwǔ jié de shíhòu, wǒmen chī zòngzi.
examples.english: During the Dragon Boat Festival, we eat rice dumplings.

examples.chinese: 她亲手包了很多粽子(她親手包了很多粽子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā qīnshǒu bāo le hěn duō zòngzi.
examples.english: She made many rice dumplings by hand.

examples.chinese: 粽子有甜的(粽子有甜的)，也有咸的(也有鹹的)。
examples.pinyin: Zòngzi yǒu tián de, yě yǒu xián de.
examples.english: Rice dumplings can be sweet or savory.
